generatedSentence: Frankly, the lovely nymph, known for her monkish habits, was warned; she outlived her usefulness. Fishermen often saw her rocking chair, a familiar arch in her home, exiting with a jean jacket. Her only wish was for more truffle eggnog, but her database of rare inventories, frankly, nevermore burst with it. She’d received kudos for her orphrey work, but her mistress now saw her as tonnage, a mere flick of the wrist away from being a limpkin. At sunset, a bumpy kickoff began when a gangster, previously asleep and known for mayhem, initiated a daring jailbreak. He'd damaged his old home, now a dumper, and his landlord would never justify his option to update it. He planned to hijack a vehicle, adjusting a chalky hockey mallet he held. He sought a lass, his vivacious mistress, but his curt reply regarding the volumes of other stolen goods left her hearts cold. The vivacious lass, a veejay with a wacky, matching jacket, had received a kiss and then been warned. She used her processor to update the database, verifying the ominous link to the gangster’s jailbreak. Her plan was to sponsor a charity event as a barrier, causing a diversion. She adjusted her handmade wallet, contemplating the rickety ski lift leading to the overgrown office where he kept his hidden union funds, inside a lagoon-side paddock, casting a long shadow. Eventually, the jogger, a noble schmuck, found himself on the outskirts, his imagination running wild as he thwacked a hornet’s nest. A dumper full of tallow, selling its contents, blocked the entrance. The gangster, now feeling a sharp resonance in his abdomen, faced an onslaught of luminous, mystical vultures near a donkey, illuminated by the length of a fathomless whirlpool. He had nowhere to go, his dream of other profits now just napkins.
panelSentences: Frankly, the lovely nymph, known for her monkish habits, was warned; she outlived her usefulness. Fishermen often saw her rocking chair, a familiar arch in her home, exiting with a jean jacket. Her only wish was for more truffle eggnog, but her database of rare inventories, frankly, nevermore burst with it. She’d received kudos for her orphrey work, but her mistress now saw her as tonnage, a mere flick of the wrist away from being a limpkin.

At sunset, a bumpy kickoff began when a gangster, previously asleep and known for mayhem, initiated a daring jailbreak. He'd damaged his old home, now a dumper, and his landlord would never justify his option to update it. He planned to hijack a vehicle, adjusting a chalky hockey mallet he held. He sought a lass, his vivacious mistress, but his curt reply regarding the volumes of other stolen goods left her hearts cold.

The vivacious lass, a veejay with a wacky, matching jacket, had received a kiss and then been warned. She used her processor to update the database, verifying the ominous link to the gangster’s jailbreak. Her plan was to sponsor a charity event as a barrier, causing a diversion. She adjusted her handmade wallet, contemplating the rickety ski lift leading to the overgrown office where he kept his hidden union funds, inside a lagoon-side paddock, casting a long shadow.

Eventually, the jogger, a noble schmuck, found himself on the outskirts, his imagination running wild as he thwacked a hornet’s nest. A dumper full of tallow, selling its contents, blocked the entrance. The gangster, now feeling a sharp resonance in his abdomen, faced an onslaught of luminous, mystical vultures near a donkey, illuminated by the length of a fathomless whirlpool. He had nowhere to go, his dream of other profits now just napkins.
rawPanelSentences: Frankly, the lovely nymph, known for her monkish habits, was warned; she outlived her usefulness. Fishermen often saw her rocking chair, a familiar arch in her home, exiting with a jean jacket. Her only wish was for more truffle eggnog, but her database of rare inventories, frankly, nevermore burst with it. She’d received kudos for her orphrey work, but her mistress now saw her as tonnage, a mere flick of the wrist away from being a limpkin.

At sunset, a bumpy kickoff began when a gangster, previously asleep and known for mayhem, initiated a daring jailbreak. He'd damaged his old home, now a dumper, and his landlord would never justify his option to update it. He planned to hijack a vehicle, adjusting a chalky hockey mallet he held. He sought a lass, his vivacious mistress, but his curt reply regarding the volumes of other stolen goods left her hearts cold.

The vivacious lass, a veejay with a wacky, matching jacket, had received a kiss and then been warned. She used her processor to update the database, verifying the ominous link to the gangster’s jailbreak. Her plan was to sponsor a charity event as a barrier, causing a diversion. She adjusted her handmade wallet, contemplating the rickety ski lift leading to the overgrown office where he kept his hidden union funds, inside a lagoon-side paddock, casting a long shadow.

Eventually, the jogger, a noble schmuck, found himself on the outskirts, his imagination running wild as he thwacked a hornet’s nest. A dumper full of tallow, selling its contents, blocked the entrance. The gangster, now feeling a sharp resonance in his abdomen, faced an onslaught of luminous, mystical vultures near a donkey, illuminated by the length of a fathomless whirlpool. He had nowhere to go, his dream of other profits now just napkins.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A protagonist with a glowing cybernetic arm and a flowing trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist standing on a futuristic archway, looking at a database projected in the air, low-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Antagonist, a gangster, breaking out of a high-tech jail cell, mid-shot, dynamic action. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a high-tech office, looking at a map of a lagoon and a ski lift, over-the-shoulder shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Antagonist surrounded by glowing cyber-vultures in a desolate, neon-lit landscape, wide shot.

definitions.definition: Praise; accolades.

The stick used to strike the ball in the sport of polo.

To impair the soundness, goodness, or value of; to harm or cause destruction.
definitions.example: Be careful not to damage any of the fragile items while unpacking them.

definitions.definition: To undergo damage.
Suffered a damage.
A person who owns and rents land such as a house, apartment, or condo.
The owner or manager of a public house.
(with "the") A shark, imagined as the owner of the surf to be avoided.
The opening kick of each half of a game of football.
(by extension) the opening sequence of any event
An other, another (person, etc), more often rendered as another.
definitions.example: I'm afraid little Robbie does not always play well with others.

definitions.definition: The other one; the second of two.
definitions.example: One boat is not better than the other.

definitions.definition: To regard, label or treat as an "other", as not part of the same group; to view as different and alien.
To treat as different or separate; segregate; ostracise.
See other (determiner) below
Second.
definitions.example: I get paid every other week.

definitions.definition: Alien.
Different.
Left, as opposed to right.
Apart from; in the phrase "other than".
definitions.example: Other than that, I'm fine.

definitions.definition: Otherwise.
definitions.example: If you think other. — Shakespeare.

definitions.definition: A jerk; a person who is unlikable, detestable, or contemptible because he or she is stupid, foolish, clumsy, oafish, inept, malicious, or unpleasant.
A deplorable, pitiful person; often in the form poor schmuck.
To substantiate or prove the truth of something
To confirm or test the truth or accuracy of something
To affirm something formally, under oath
Someone who presents a television programme of videos; a video jockey.
To work as a video jockey; to present videos.
Lively and animated; full of life and energy.
Long-lived.
Difficult to kill.
To touch with the lips or press the lips against, usually to show love or affection or passion, or as part of a greeting.
To (cause to) touch lightly or slightly; to come into contact.
definitions.example: His ball kissed the black into the corner pocket.

definitions.definition: Of two or more people, to touch each other's lips together, usually to express love or affection or passion.
To treat with fondness.
To agree; to be equal; to correspond.
definitions.example: Their interests didn't match, so it took a long time to agree what to do together.

definitions.definition: To agree with; to be equal to; to correspond to.
definitions.example: His interests didn't match her interests.

definitions.definition: To make a successful match or pairing.
definitions.example: They found out about his color-blindness when he couldn't match socks properly.

definitions.definition: To equal or exceed in achievement.
definitions.example: She matched him at every turn: anything he could do, she could do as well or better.

definitions.definition: To unite in marriage, to mate.
To fit together, or make suitable for fitting together; specifically, to furnish with a tongue and groove at the edges.
definitions.example: to match boards

definitions.definition: The process by which things are matched together or paired up.
A set of independent edges in a given graph, i.e. a set of edges which do not intersect, such that pairs of vertices are "matched" to each other one to one.
The same as another; sharing the same design.
definitions.example: We bought a new sofa and matching armchairs.

definitions.definition: Relating to mystics or mysticism.
Having a spiritual or magical significance that transcends human understanding.
Inspiring a sense of spiritual mystery, awe, and fascination.
Any of a group of some twenty thousand species of vascular plants classified in the division Pteridophyta that lack seeds and reproduce by shedding spores to initiate an alternation of generations.
The act of thwacking; a strike or blow, especially with a flat implement.
A heavy slapping sound.
To hit with a flat implement.
To beat.
To fill to overflow.
Someone thought to have an unusual degree of enlightenment.
To shine light on something.
To decorate something with lights.
To clarify or make something understandable.
To decorate the page of a manuscript book with ornamental designs.
To make spectacular.
To glow; to light up.
To be exposed to light.
To direct a radar beam toward.
Enlightened
Any of several carrion-eating birds of the families Accipitridae and Cathartidae.
A person who profits from the suffering of others.
definitions.example: Within ten minutes of the accident, the vultures appeared and were organizing lawsuits.

definitions.definition: To circle around one's target as if one were a vulture.
definitions.example: Rudy vultured when asking the girl out.

definitions.definition: Zany; eccentric
A piece of clothing worn on the upper body outside a shirt or blouse, often waist length to thigh length.
A piece of a person's suit, beside trousers and, sometimes, waistcoat; coat (US)
A protective or insulating cover for an object (e.g. a book, hot water tank, bullet.)
A police record.
In ordnance, a strengthening band surrounding and reinforcing the tube in which the charge is fired.
The tough outer skin of a baked potato.
definitions.example: Cook the potatoes in their jackets.

definitions.definition: To enclose or encase in a jacket or other covering.
To grow beyond one's boundaries or containment, or beyond the proper size.
To grow over; (of one thing) to cause (a second thing) to become overgrown (with or by the first thing).
Having large numbers of plants which have become too big, and are hence spoiling the picturesqueness of a garden.
Having grown bigger without developing maturity or changing character.
definitions.example: He spends so much money on new technology: he's like an overgrown schoolboy buying toys.

definitions.definition: A person or organisation with some sort of responsibility for another person or organisation, especially where the responsibility has a religious, legal, or financial aspect.
definitions.example: He was my sponsor when I applied to join the club.

definitions.definition: One that pays all or part of the cost of an event, a publication, or a media program, usually in exchange for advertising time.
definitions.example: And now a word from our sponsor.

definitions.definition: To be a sponsor for.
A device that transports skiers up a mountain.
A sharp pinch or jerk; a twist or twitch.
definitions.example: a tweak of the nose

definitions.definition: A slight adjustment or modification.
definitions.example: He is running so many tweaks it is hard to remember how it looked originally.

definitions.definition: Trouble; distress; tweag.
A prostitute.
An additional input to a block cipher, used in conjunction with the key to select the permutation computed by the cipher.
To pinch and pull with a sudden jerk and twist; to twitch.
definitions.example: to tweak the nose.

definitions.definition: To adjust slightly; to fine-tune.
definitions.example: If we tweak the colors towards blue, it will look more natural.

definitions.definition: To twit or tease.
To abuse methamphetamines, especially crystal meth.
To exhibit symptoms of methamphetamine abuse, such as extreme nervousness, compulsiveness, erratic motion, excitability; possibly a blend of twitch and freak.
To exhibit extreme nervousness, evasiveness when confronted by law enforcement or other authority (e.g., customs agents, border patrol, teacher, etc.), mimicking methamphetamine abuse symptoms.
A structure that bars passage.
An obstacle or impediment.
A boundary or limit.
(grammar) A node (in government and binding theory) said to intervene between other nodes A and B if it is a potential governor for B, c-commands B, and does not c-command A.
A separation between two areas of the body where specialized cells allow the entry of certain substances but prevent the entry of others.
The lists in a tournament.
(in the plural) A martial exercise of the 15th and 16th centuries.
To block or obstruct with a barrier.
A shallow body of water separated from deeper sea by a bar.
Some text or a graphic in an electronic document that can be activated to display another document or trigger an action.
definitions.example: Click the hyperlink to go to the next page.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An address, URL, or program that defines a hyperlink's function.
definitions.example: Copy the hyperlink and paste it into an email.

definitions.definition: A connection between places, people, events, things, or ideas.
definitions.example: The mayor’s assistant serves as the link to the media.

definitions.definition: One element of a chain or other connected series.
definitions.example: The third link of the silver chain needs to be resoldered.

definitions.definition: The connection between buses or systems.
definitions.example: A by-N-link is composed of N lanes.

definitions.definition: A space comprising one or more disjoint knots.
A thin wild bank of land splitting two cultivated patches and often linking two hills.
An individual person or element in a system
Anything doubled and closed like a link of a chain.
definitions.example: a link of horsehair

definitions.definition: A sausage that is not a patty.

(kinematics) Any one of the several elementary pieces of a mechanism, such as the fixed frame, or a rod, wheel, mass of confined liquid, etc., by which relative motion of other parts is produced and constrained.

Any intermediate rod or piece for transmitting force or motion, especially a short connecting rod with a bearing at each end; specifically (in steam engines) the slotted bar, or connecting piece, to the opposite ends of which the eccentric rods are jointed, and by means of which the movement of the valve is varied, in a link motion.
The length of one joint of Gunter's chain, being the hundredth part of it, or 7.92 inches, the chain being 66 feet in length.
A bond of affinity, or a unit of valence between atoms; applied to a unit of chemical force or attraction.
(plural) The windings of a river; the land along a winding stream.
To connect two or more things.
(of a Web page) To contain a hyperlink to another page.
definitions.example: My homepage links to my wife's.

definitions.definition: To supply (somebody) with a hyperlink; to direct by means of a link.
definitions.example: Haven't you seen his Web site? I'll link you to it.

definitions.definition: To post a hyperlink to.
definitions.example: Stop linking those unfunny comics all the time!

definitions.definition: To demonstrate a correlation between two things.
To combine objects generated by a compiler into a single executable.
The interior or inner part.
definitions.example: The inside of the building has been extensively restored.

definitions.definition: The left-hand side of a road if one drives on the left, or right-hand side if one drives on the right.
definitions.example: On a motorway, you should never pass another vehicle on the inside.

definitions.definition: The side of a curved road, racetrack etc. that has the shorter arc length; the side of a racetrack nearer the interior of the course or some other point of reference.
definitions.example: The car in front drifted wide on the bend, so I darted up the inside to take the lead.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) The interior organs of the body, especially the guts.
definitions.example: Eating that stuff will damage your insides.

definitions.definition: A passenger within a coach or carriage, as distinguished from one upon the outside.
Of or pertaining to the inner surface, limit or boundary.
definitions.example: The inside surface of the cup is unpainted.

definitions.definition: Nearer to the interior or centre of something.
definitions.example: All the window seats were occupied, so she took an inside seat.

definitions.definition: Originating from, arranged by, or being someone inside an organisation.
definitions.example: The reporter had received inside information about the forthcoming takeover.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Legally married to or related to (e.g. born in wedlock to), and/or residing with, a specified other person (parent, child, or partner); (of a marriage, relationship, etc) existing between two such people.
(of a pitch) Toward the batter as it crosses home plate.
definitions.example: The first pitch is ... just a bit inside.

definitions.definition: At or towards or the left-hand side of the road if one drives on the left, or right-hand side if one drives on the right.
definitions.example: the inside lane of the motorway

definitions.definition: Within or towards the interior of something; within the scope or limits of something (a place), especially a building.
definitions.example: It started raining, so I went inside.

definitions.definition: Indoors.
definitions.example: It was snowing, so the children stayed inside.

definitions.definition: Intimately, secretly; without expressing what one is feeling or thinking.
definitions.example: Are you laughing at us inside?

definitions.definition: Within the interior of something, closest to the center or to a specific point of reference.
definitions.example: He placed the letter inside the envelope.

definitions.definition: Within a period of time.
definitions.example: The job was finished inside two weeks.

definitions.definition: A conveyance; a device for carrying or transporting substances, objects or individuals.
A medium for expression of talent or views.
A liquid content (e.g. oil) which acts as a binding and drying agent in paint. (FM 55-501).
(pharmaceuticals) The main excipient (such as an oil or gel) that conveys the active ingredient of a drug.
An entity to achieve an end.
A mode or method of spiritual practice; a yana.
An animal or (rarely) a plant on which a Hindu deity rides or sits
Of an object: not strong or sturdy, as because of poor construction or upkeep; not safe or secure.
definitions.example: He hesitated about climbing such a small, rickety ladder.

definitions.definition: Of a person: feeble in the joints; tottering.
definitions.example: The rickety old man hardly managed to climb the stairs.

definitions.definition: Affected with or suffering from rickets.
A small case, often flat and often made of leather, for keeping money (especially paper money), credit cards, etc.
definitions.example: The thief stole all the money and credit cards out of the old man's wallet.

definitions.definition: (by extension) A person's bank account or assets.
definitions.example: It's unknown if the pro running back's recent sex scandal will hit him in the wallet or not.

definitions.definition: A thick case or folder with plastic sleeves in which compact discs may be stored.
definitions.example: I won an auction online for a cheap CD wallet.

definitions.definition: A bag or pouch.
definitions.example: He brought with him a large wallet with some provisions for the road.

definitions.definition: A person's buttocks (the area of the body nearest where one keeps one's wallet).
definitions.example: He fell down and landed on his wallet.

definitions.definition: An art or craft object made by hand.
Manufactured by hand.
definitions.example: handmade shoes

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to an omen or to omens; being or exhibiting an omen; significant.
Specifically, giving indication of a coming ill; being an evil omen
definitions.example: California poll support for Jerry Brown's tax increases has ominous implications for U.S. taxpayers too Los Angeles Times Headline April 25, 2011

definitions.definition: A ceremonial duty or service, particularly:
A position of responsibility.
definitions.example: When the office of Secretary of State is vacant, its duties fall upon an official within the department.

definitions.definition: Official position, particularly high employment within government; tenure in such a position.
definitions.example: She held office as secretary of state until she left office to run for office.

definitions.definition: An official or group of officials; a personification of officeholders.
A duty, particularly owing to one's position or station; a charge, trust, or role; moral duty.
The performance of a duty; an instance of performing a duty.
Function: anything typically done by or expected of something.
A bodily function, urination and defecation; an act of urination or defecation.
(now usually in plural) A service, a kindness.
Inside information.
A room, set of rooms, or building used for non-manual work, particularly:
definitions.example: The office of the Secretary of State is cleaned when it is vacant.

definitions.definition: The staff of such places.
definitions.example: The whole office was there... well, except you, of course.

definitions.definition: (in large organizations) The administrative departments housed in such places, particularly:
definitions.example: He's from our public relations office.

definitions.definition: (now in the plural) The parts of a house or estate devoted to manual work and storage, as the kitchen, scullery, laundry, stables, etc., particularly a house or estate's facilities for urination and defecation: outhouses or lavatories.
Clipping of inquest of office: an inquest undertaken on occasions when the Crown claimed the right of possession to land or property.
A piece of land used for hunting; the area of land overseen by a gamekeeper.
A hangout: a place where one is normally found.
A plane's cockpit, particularly an observer's cockpit.
A collection of business software typically including a word processor and spreadsheet and slideshow programs.
To provide (someone) with an office.
To have an office.
A small enclosure or field of grassland, especially for horses.
A field of grassland of any size, especially for keeping sheep or cattle.
An area where horses are paraded and mounted before a race and unsaddled after a race.
Land, fenced or otherwise delimited, which is most often part of a sheep or cattle property.
An area at circuit where the racing vehicles are parked and worked on before and between races.
(field sports) The playing field.
To provide with a paddock.
To keep in, or place in, a paddock.
A person or institution who processes things (foods, photos, applications, etc.).
definitions.example: He is a loan processor with a bank.

definitions.definition: A device which processes, which changes something (a computer processor, food processor, etc.).
definitions.example: The food processor shredded the vegetables.

definitions.definition: A central processing unit.
A microprocessor.
definitions.example: This computer has two processors, but only one keyboard.

definitions.definition: The act of uniting or joining two or more things into one.
The state of being united or joined; a state of unity or harmony.
That which is united, or made one; something formed by a combination or coalition of parts or members; a confederation; a consolidated body; a league.
A trade union; a workers' union.
An association of students at a university for social and/or political purposes; also in some cases a debating body.
A joint or other connection uniting parts of machinery, such as pipes.
The set containing all of the elements of two or more sets.
The act or state of marriage.
Sexual intercourse.
A data structure that can store any of various types of item, but only one at a time.
A large, high-quality pearl.
An affiliation of several parishes for joint support and management of their poor; also the jointly-owned workhouse.
To combine sets using the union operation.
An aristocrat; one of aristocratic blood.
definitions.example: This country house was occupied by nobles in the 16th century.

definitions.definition: A medieval gold coin of England in the 14th and 15th centuries, usually valued at 6s 8d.
Having honorable qualities; having moral eminence and freedom from anything petty, mean or dubious in conduct and character.
definitions.example: He is a noble man who would never put his family in jeopardy.

definitions.definition: Grand; stately; magnificent; splendid.
definitions.example: a noble edifice

definitions.definition: Of exalted rank; of or relating to the nobility; distinguished from the masses by birth, station, or title; highborn.
definitions.example: noble blood; a noble personage

definitions.definition: (of a polyhedron) Both isohedral and isogonal.
A serviette; a (usually rectangular) piece of cloth or paper used at the table for wiping the mouth and hands for cleanliness while eating.
A nappy (UK), a diaper (American).
A small scarf worn on the head by Christian women (chiefly Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox) when entering a church, as a token of modesty.
A sanitary napkin.
The fat surrounding the belly.
The belly, or that part of the body between the thorax and the pelvis, not including the back; or in some lower vertebrates, the portion between the cardiac and caudal regions.

The cavity of the belly, which is lined by the peritoneum, and contains the viscera; often restricted in humans to the part between the diaphragm and the commencement of the pelvis, the remainder being called the pelvic cavity.
definitions.example: He was all bent over complaining of pains in the abdomen.

definitions.definition: The posterior section of the body, behind the thorax, in insects, crustaceans, and other Arthropoda.
The image-making power of the mind; the act of mentally creating or reproducing an object not previously perceived; the ability to create such images.
definitions.example: Imagination is one of the most advanced human faculties.

definitions.definition: Particularly, construction of false images; fantasizing.
definitions.example: You think someone's been following you? That's just your imagination.

definitions.definition: Creativity; resourcefulness.
definitions.example: His imagination makes him a valuable team member.

definitions.definition: A mental image formed by the action of the imagination as a faculty; something imagined.
A member of a criminal or street gang.
A member of a professional criminal organization; a racketeer.
To act like a gangster; to commit street crime or run a racket.
A swirling body of water.
definitions.example: A whirlpool is an instance of a vortex produced by ocean tides, or by a hole underneath where the water would drain out, such as in a bathtub.

definitions.definition: A hot tub, jacuzzi.
Turmoil, or agitated excitement.
To spin or swirl like the water in a whirlpool.
A person who jogs (as exercise).
A tracksuit, particularly the trousers.
A printing press operator who removes, jogs, and stacks the sheets or signatures of paper.
(chiefly derogatory) A black person.
The area surrounding a city or town; suburb.
definitions.example: He lived on the outskirts of Paris for a time.

definitions.definition: A domestic animal, Equus asinus asinus, similar to a horse
A stubborn person
A fool
A small auxiliary engine
A bad poker player
(ditransitive) To transfer goods or provide services in exchange for money.
definitions.example: I'll sell you all three for a hundred dollars.

definitions.definition: To be sold.
definitions.example: The corn sold for a good price.

definitions.definition: To promote a product or service.
To promote a particular viewpoint.
definitions.example: My boss is very old-fashioned and I'm having a lot of trouble selling the idea of working at home occasionally.

definitions.definition: To betray for money.
To trick, cheat, or manipulate someone.
To pretend that an opponent's blows or maneuvers are causing legitimate injury; to act.
Action of the verb to sell.
definitions.example: buyings and sellings

definitions.definition: Skill at salesmanship.
definitions.example: You've got to work on your selling.

definitions.definition: Very deep (especially of water deeper than a lead line can measure); bottomless.
(by extension) unfathomable or incomprehensible.
No particular place, noplace.
definitions.example: They went on a cruise to nowhere.

definitions.definition: Unimportant; unworthy of notice.
In no place.
definitions.example: Nowhere did the rules say anything about popcorn.

definitions.definition: To no place.
definitions.example: We sat in traffic, going nowhere.

definitions.definition: An organization, the objective of which is to carry out a charitable purpose.
The goods or money given to those in need.
Benevolence to others less fortunate than ourselves; the providing of goods or money to those in need.
In general, an attitude of kindness and understanding towards others, now especially suggesting generosity.
Christian love; representing God's love of man, man's love of God, or man's love of his fellow-men.
The distance measured along the longest dimension of an object.
Duration.
The length of a horse, used to indicate the distance between horses at the end of a race.
Distance between the two ends of a line segment.
The distance down the pitch that the ball bounces on its way to the batsman.
Total extent.
definitions.example: the length of a book

definitions.definition: Part of something that is long; a physical piece of something.
definitions.example: a length of rope

definitions.definition: To lengthen.
The quality of being resonant.
A resonant sound, echo, or reverberation, such as that produced by blowing over the top of a bottle.
The sound produced by a hollow body part such as the chest cavity upon auscultation, especially that produced while the patient is speaking.
Something that evokes an association, or a strong emotion.
The increase in the amplitude of an oscillation of a system under the influence of a periodic force whose frequency is close to that of the system's natural frequency.
A short-lived subatomic particle or state of atomic excitation that results from the collision of atomic particles.
definitions.example: 2004, When experiments with the first ‘atom-smashers’ took place in the 1950s to 1960s, many short-lived heavier siblings of the proton and neutron, known as ‘resonances’, were discovered. — Frank Close, Particle Physics: A Very Short Introduction (Oxford 2004, p. 35)

definitions.definition: An increase in the strength or duration of a musical tone produced by sympathetic vibration.
The property of a compound that can be visualized as having two structures differing only in the distribution of electrons; mesomerism.
A influence of the gravitational forces of one orbiting object on the orbit of another, causing periodic perturbations.
The condition where the inductive and capacitive reactances have equal magnitude.
In the end; at some later time, especially after a long time, a series of problems, struggles, delays or setbacks.
(of a sequence) For some tail; for all terms beyond some term; with only finitely many exceptions.
definitions.example: Eventually, all prime numbers are odd.

definitions.definition: A large wasp, of the genus Vespa, having a brown-and-yellow-striped body and the ability to inflict a serious sting.
A person who pesters with petty but ceaseless attacks.
A fierce attack.
A large number of people or things resembling an attack.
definitions.example: They opened the doors and prepared for the onslaught of holiday shoppers.
